title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman achieved a 2.10 ERA with 17 saves and a reliever strikeouts record in the current season.
Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA, will return to Philadelphia for his second All-Star Game appearance.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but understanding over Jaylen Brown's trade from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Pierce, who was similarly traded in 2013, encouraged Brown to use the trade as motivation for his career.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by Boston, highlighting past instances of being overlooked, such as Olympic and All-NBA selections.

title: Photos: Boston kicks off Fourth of July celebration
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: The 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence began with a parade and reading in Boston.
Crowds gathered outside the Old State House for the reading led by city officials including Mayor Michelle Wu.
Festivities continued into the evening on the Esplanade, attracting people like Anna and Jacob Melendez from California.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the last 30 days.
Durbin's performance improved after working with a new hitting coach, excelling at third base.
Boston has team control over Durbin until 2031, offering roster flexibility and cap benefits.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman of the Boston Red Sox set a new MLB record for relief pitcher strikeouts with 1,364.
Chapman broke Hoyt Wilhelm's record during a 5-2 victory over the Los Angeles Angels in Anaheim.
Despite his record achievement, Chapman may be traded as Boston evaluates its playoff chances with a 13% likelihood.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Allstate's 2026 report ranks Boston as the most collision-prone city, with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, rank among the lowest for safe driving, with intervals of 5.14 and 5.18 years between collisions.
Providence, RI, is among the top 10 collision-prone cities, with an average of 5.87 years between crashes.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led troopers on a 105 mph chase over the Bourne Bridge, initiating from Route 25 eastbound.
Deleon faces over a dozen charges, including assault with a dangerous weapon and obstructing an emergency vehicle.
Deleon was held without bail after barricading himself at home; his pretrial hearing is set for August 5.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released photos of a juvenile suspect in a June 10 armed robbery at a lemonade stand.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested and arraigned for armed robbery and firearm possession in this case.
Public assistance is requested to identify the second suspect; tips can be submitted through Boston Police CrimeStoppers.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering new NBA teams, signaling a departure from the Lakers after eight seasons.
Rich Paul mentioned Boston Celtics' Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens as potential, but less likely, options for James.
James joining Boston could create a strong lineup with Derrick White, Paul George, Tatum, and Mitchell Robinson.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers, comparing it to a past controversial trade.
Parish expressed that he found the trade rumors and decision unsettling, suggesting it would not have pleased Red Auerbach.
Despite disapproval, Parish hopes Brown and the Celtics maintain a positive relationship and anticipates Brown's future jersey retirement.
